title: QA: hook input serialization failure silently sends empty stdin (run)
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/hooks/mod.rs:411
## Violation
The hook input passed to every command hook is serialized with a
swallowed error:
```rust
let input_json = serde_json::to_string(input).unwrap_or_default();
```
If `HookInput` ever fails to serialize (custom serializer panic, locale
issue, etc.), `unwrap_or_default()` produces `String::default()` =
empty string. Hooks then receive `""` on stdin and have no way to know
the input was lost — they may default-allow on missing fields or hang
waiting for data. The actual error is silently discarded; no `tracing`
entry, no metric, no audit-log row.
## Mandated refactor
Replace with explicit error handling: on serialization failure, log
`error!(error = %e, "Failed to serialize HookInput; refusing to run
hook")` and return `HookResult::denied(...)` for pre-tool-use hooks (or
emit a dedicated error variant for post-tool hooks). Better: change the
hook contract so serialization happens once at HookEngine startup or
construct-time, and serialization errors propagate up at config-load
time rather than per-call.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
